location: Rov Basin and old canal cities
contradiction: They worship lawful order, yet nobles weaponize loopholes and forged descent records.

enemy_insults: Cairn dogs; frost debtors; bog kings
location: Northern iron hills, bog forts and cold forests
contradiction: They praise freedom from empire, but clan obligation can trap people harder than any imperial tax office.

enemy_insults: Grass thieves; saddle ghosts; treaty burners
location: Eastern steppe, river camps and border markets
contradiction: They claim no wall can own them, but herd law and clan discipline can be brutally strict.

enemy_insults: Salt judges; cup kings; contract snakes
location: Oasis chains, salt roads and hot stone towns
contradiction: They call hospitality sacred, but survival makes them ruthless about debt, water and exclusion.

location: Lanter Sea ports, islands, river mouths and lighthouse cities
contradiction: They praise liberty while binding poor debtors, sailors and immigrants with contracts almost as hard as serfdom.

meaning: people of the kept place or inner oath
location: Greyhook Spine, mine cities, bridgeholds and tunnel roads
contradiction: They claim they hate kings, but vault councils can rule workers with king-like power.

meaning: continuous households or people of long obligation
location: Old embassies, river courts, charter districts, groves and ancient island cities
contradiction: They preach long responsibility, but ancient contracts can strangle younger peoples for generations.

location: Border farms, hard valleys, old military colonies, river margins and steppe edges
contradiction: They despise being treated as weapons, yet many freeholds survive by selling military service.

enemy_insults: Wheat lords; saddle saints; cousin kings
location: Western grain plains and cavalry estates
contradiction: They preach honour while treating marriage, inheritance and oath as weapons.

meaning: the peoples bound under shared banners
location: Sacred battlefields, temple districts and disputed frontier towns
contradiction: They promise unity under sacred law, but every joined people remembers a different wound.
